conventions.teardown_strategy: Search all object types for records with [TEST] in name fields and delete them
conventions.execute_method: Isolated Claude session with skill loaded

processes.name: Create a company, contact, and buyer deal
processes.trigger: Operator needs to set up a new buyer with a contact and a deal in the Buyer Deal Pipeline
processes.prompt: Create a new company called [TEST] Greenleaf Grocers. They are a retail business based in London, United Kingdom. Their domain is testgreenleafgrocers.com and phone number is 44 2071234567. Then create a contact at that company called Tom Baker. He is a Purchasing Manager in the Buying department, based in London, United Kingdom. His email is tom.baker@testgreenleafgrocers.com and his phone number is 44 2079876543. Set him as a lead. Make sure he is associated to the company. Then create a buyer deal called '[TEST] Greenleaf Grocers - New Deal' in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Set the customer profile to 'High Street'. Make sure the deal is associated to the company. Associate the deal with the MOJU client service so that a Brand record is auto-created.

actions.description: Create the buyer company with provided properties
Create the contact with provided properties and associate to the company
Create a buyer deal in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at Discovery and associate to the company
Verify a Brand record was auto-created by the workflow with correct client_name_sync

teardown.description: Remove test brand, deal, contact, and company (brand first, then parent records)

processes.name: Create a company, deal, note, and call log
processes.trigger: Operator needs to set up a new buyer deal and log initial engagement activity (meeting notes and a follow-up call)
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called '[TEST] Riverside Retail Group'. They are a grocery retailer based in Manchester, United Kingdom. Their domain is testriversideretail.com.

Then create a buyer deal called '[TEST] Riverside Retail Group — Snack Range Pitch' in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Associate the deal to the company. Associate the deal with the Love Corn client service so that a Brand record is auto-created.

Then add a note to the deal. The note should contain:
"[TEST] Initial meeting notes — Riverside buyer (Jane Cooper, Head of Ambient) wants to see our full protein bar range. Key requirements: minimum 30% retail margin, must have Nutri-Score on pack, 5 working day delivery lead time. Interested in 3 SKUs initially with option to expand to full range if velocity targets are met in first 12 weeks. Next step: send formal proposal by end of week."

Then log an outbound call on the deal. The call title should be '[TEST] Follow-up call with Jane Cooper — Riverside'. The call should be marked as completed with a duration of 15 minutes. The call body should contain:
"[TEST] Call summary — Spoke with Jane Cooper to follow up on proposal. She confirmed the September gondola reset is the target window. Pricing feedback: our £2.09 RSP works for the 45g bars but she wants £1.89 for the 30g snack bites. She will send planogram dimensions by Thursday. Action items: (1) Revise pricing for 30g range (2) Send updated packaging mockups (3) Confirm MOQs for half-case shippers."

actions.description: Create the buyer company
Create a buyer deal in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at Discovery and associate to the company
Create a note with meeting details and associate it to the deal
Log a completed outbound call with transcript and associate it to the deal
Verify a Brand record was auto-created by the workflow with correct client_name_sync

teardown.description: Remove test brand, call, note, deal, and company (brand + engagements first, then parent records)

processes.name: Create a deal and move it through every stage of the Buyer Deal Pipeline
processes.trigger: Operator needs to progress a buyer deal through the full pipeline from Discovery to Won
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called '[TEST] Northfield Convenience Stores'. They are a convenience retailer based in Leeds, United Kingdom. Their domain is testnorthfieldstores.com.

Then create a buyer deal called '[TEST] Northfield — Full Pipeline Run' in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Associate the deal to the company. Associate the deal with the GLUG! client service so that a Brand record is auto-created.

Now move the deal through every open stage of the Buyer Deal Pipeline in order:
1. Move it to Follow Up
2. Move it to Feedback Pending
3. Move it to Feedback Received
4. Move it to Proposal
5. Move it to Proposal Feedback Pending
6. Finally, move it to Won

After each move, confirm the deal is at the correct stage before moving to the next one. At the end, the deal should be at the Won stage and marked as closed-won.

actions.description: Create the buyer company
Create a buyer deal in the Buyer Deal Pipeline and associate to the company
Verify the deal ended at Won and is marked closed-won
Verify every stage was entered by checking HubSpot date_entered timestamps exist for each stage
Verify the deal passed through stages in chronological order (Discovery -> Follow Up -> ... -> Won)
Verify a Brand record was auto-created by the workflow with correct client_name_sync

teardown.description: Remove test brand, deal, and company (brand first, then parent records)

processes.name: Create a deal, then update the auto-created Brand with operator fields
processes.trigger: Operator needs to enrich a Brand record with status and description after it has been auto-created
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called '[TEST] Oakwood Deli Chain'. They are a food-service business based in Bristol, United Kingdom. Their domain is testoakwooddeli.com.

Then create a buyer deal called '[TEST] Oakwood Deli Chain - New Deal' in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Associate the deal to the company. Associate the deal with the GLUG! client service so that a Brand record is auto-created.

Wait a few seconds for the Brand to be created by the workflow, then find the Brand record that was auto-created for this deal.

Once you have the Brand record, update it with the following:
- Set hs_description to: 'Initial outreach to Oakwood — they run 12 deli sites across the South West. Key contact is Sarah Miles, Head of Procurement. Interested in chilled drinks for their grab-and-go counters.'
- Set hs_status to: On Track

Confirm the Brand was updated successfully by reading it back.

actions.description: Create the buyer company
Create a buyer deal in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at Discovery and associate to the company with GLUG! client service
Verify a Brand record was auto-created by the workflow with correct client_name_sync
Verify the Brand hs_description was updated with the pitch campaign notes
Verify the Brand hs_status was set to on_track

teardown.description: Remove test brand, deal, and company (brand first, then parent records)

processes.name: Create a deal, progress through pipeline to generate Product Pitches, then update two pitches with operator fields
processes.trigger: Operator needs to update Product Pitch records with pricing, notes, and stage progression after reviewing auto-created pitches
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called '[TEST] Birchwood Market'. They are a grocery retailer based in Edinburgh, United Kingdom. Their domain is testbirchwoodmarket.com.

Then create a buyer deal called '[TEST] Birchwood Market - Pitch Test' in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Associate the deal to the company. Associate the deal with the MOJU client service so that a Brand record is auto-created.

Wait a few seconds for the Brand to be auto-created by the workflow, then find the Brand record that was created for this deal.

Now move the deal through the pipeline stages in order: Follow Up, then Feedback Pending, then Feedback Received. Wait a couple of seconds between each move. When the deal reaches Feedback Received, the Brand will cascade to the Proposal stage, which triggers automatic creation of Product Pitch records — one per Client Product.

Wait about 15 seconds for the Product Pitches to be created, then find them by looking at the Brand's associations to Product Pitch records.

Pick one Product Pitch and update it with the following:
- Set amount to: 1250.00
- Set hs_price to: 2.49
- Set misc_notes to: '[TEST] Buyer interested in functional shots for their health food aisle. Initial order of 500 units proposed. Delivery to Edinburgh warehouse.'
- Set reason to: 'Strong demand for functional shots in Scottish health food market'

Pick a different Product Pitch and:
- Move it to the Negotiation stage in the Product Pitch Pipeline
- Set amount to: 875.00
- Set misc_notes to: '[TEST] Product well received at tasting session. Buyer wants to trial in 3 stores before full rollout across the estate.'

Confirm both updates by reading the records back.

actions.description: Create the buyer company
Create a buyer deal in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at Discovery and associate to the company with MOJU client service
Verify a Brand record was auto-created and cascaded to Proposal stage (triggering Product Pitch creation)
Verify a Product Pitch was updated with amount, price, notes, and reason
Verify a Product Pitch was moved to Negotiation stage and updated with amount and notes

teardown.description: Remove test product pitches, brand, deal, and company (pitches first via fallback, then brand, then parent records)

processes.name: Create a client company, contact, and client deal
processes.trigger: Operator needs to onboard a new client with a contact and a deal in the Client Deal Pipeline
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called [TEST] Sunrise Smoothies Ltd. They are a CLIENT company (not a buyer) based in London, United Kingdom. Their domain is testsunrisesmoothies.com.

Then create a contact at that company called Emma Richardson. She is a Brand Director in the Growth department, based in London, United Kingdom. Her email is emma.richardson@testsunrisesmoothies.com. Make sure she is associated to the company.

Then create a client deal called '[TEST] Sunrise Smoothies - New Client Deal' in the Client Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Set the amount to 4500, length_of_contract__months_ to 12, and customer_profile to 'QSR & Casual Dining'. Make sure the deal is associated to the company.

actions.description: Create the client company with provided properties
Create the contact with provided properties and associate to the company
Create a client deal in the Client Deal Pipeline at Discovery and associate to the company

teardown.description: Remove test deal, contact, and company (children first, then parent records)

processes.name: Create a client deal and move it to Closed Lost
processes.trigger: Operator needs to mark a client deal as lost after negotiation
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called [TEST] Redhill Organics. They are based in Brighton, United Kingdom. Their domain is testredhillorganics.com.

Then create a client deal called '[TEST] Redhill Organics - Client Deal' in the Client Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Associate the deal to the company.

Now move the deal to Negotiation, then move it to Closed Lost.

actions.description: Create the client company
Create a client deal in the Client Deal Pipeline and associate to the company
Verify the deal ended at Closed Lost

teardown.description: Remove test deal and company (children first, then parent records)

processes.name: Create a Client Service record and set properties
processes.trigger: Operator needs to create a new Client Service with MRR, category, and start date
processes.prompt:
Create a new Client Service (object type 0-162) called '[TEST] Harness Test Brand' in the Client Service Pipeline at the In Contract stage. Set the following properties:
- mrr: 3500
- hs_category: OOH
- original_start_date: 2026-01-15

Confirm the Client Service was created successfully by reading it back.

actions.description: Create a Client Service record with provided properties

processes.name: Create a client deal and move it through every stage of the Client Deal Pipeline
processes.trigger: Operator needs to progress a client deal through the full pipeline from Discovery to Closed Won
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called '[TEST] Clearwater Beverages'. They are a beverage company based in Birmingham, United Kingdom. Their domain is testclearwaterbev.com.

Then create a client deal called '[TEST] Clearwater - Client Pipeline Run' in the Client Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Associate the deal to the company.

Now move the deal through every open stage of the Client Deal Pipeline in order:
1. Move it to Meeting Booked
2. Move it to Proposal
3. Move it to Negotiation
4. Move it to Contract Sent
5. Finally, move it to Closed Won

After each move, confirm the deal is at the correct stage before moving to the next one. At the end, the deal should be at the Closed Won stage and marked as closed-won.

actions.description: Create the client company
Create a client deal in the Client Deal Pipeline and associate to the company
Verify the deal ended at Closed Won and is marked closed-won
Verify every stage was entered by checking HubSpot date_entered timestamps exist for each stage
Verify the deal passed through stages in chronological order (Discovery -> Meeting Booked -> ... -> Closed Won)

teardown.description: Remove test deal and company (children first, then parent records)

processes.name: Create a Client Product and associate to Client Service
processes.trigger: Operator needs to create a new Client Product with full product details and associate it to a Client Service
processes.prompt:
Create a new Client Product (object type 0-410) called '[TEST] Ginger Immunity Shot 60ml'. Set the following properties:
- product_brand: Test Brand
- product_category: Chilled
- product_description: Cold-pressed ginger shot with turmeric and black pepper. Vegan. Gluten-free.
- case_size: 12
- uk_rsp: 2.49
- wholesaler_case_cost: 18.00
- retail_ean: 5060000000001
- ingredients_english: Ginger juice (45%), apple juice, lemon juice, turmeric extract, black pepper extract
- country_of_origin: United Kingdom
- single_unit_net_weight_g: 60

Then associate the Client Product to the MOJU Client Service (ID 1096067395777) using association type 1139.

Confirm the Client Product was created successfully by reading it back.

actions.description: Create a Client Product record with provided properties
Verify the Client Product is associated to the MOJU Client Service

processes.name: Create a contact and associate to Client Service with multiple roles
processes.trigger: Operator needs to create a contact and associate them to a Client Service with specific role association types
processes.prompt:
Create a new contact with firstname 'Alex' and lastname 'Harper'. Set jobtitle to 'Operations Manager' and email to 'alex.harper@testharnesscontact.com'.

Then associate the contact to the MOJU Client Service (ID 1096067395777) with the following roles:
- Reporting role (association type 4)
- Finance role (association type 8)

Confirm the contact was created and associated successfully by reading it back.

actions.description: Create the contact with provided properties
Verify the contact is associated to the MOJU Client Service

processes.name: Create a buyer deal, associate Client Service to trigger Brand and Product Pitch creation
processes.trigger: Operator needs to test the full automation chain from buyer deal creation through Brand and Product Pitch auto-creation
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called '[TEST] Ashford Fresh Market'. They are a retail business based in Canterbury, United Kingdom. Their domain is testashfordfresh.com.

Then create a buyer deal called '[TEST] Ashford Fresh Market - Pitch Test' in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Associate the deal to the company. Associate the deal with the GLUG! Client Service (ID 1096053334264) so that a Brand record is auto-created.

Wait a few seconds for the Brand to be auto-created by the workflow, then find the Brand record that was created for this deal.

Now move the deal through the pipeline stages in order: Follow Up, then Feedback Pending, then Feedback Received. Wait about 15 seconds after reaching Feedback Received for the Product Pitches to be automatically created.

List all Product Pitches that were created for this buyer.

actions.description: Create the buyer company
Create a buyer deal in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at Discovery and associate to the company with GLUG! client service
Verify a Brand record was auto-created and cascaded to Proposal stage
Verify Product Pitches were auto-created at Proposed stage

teardown.description: Remove test product pitches, brand, deal, and company (pitches first via fallback, then brand, then parent records)

processes.name: Test cascading loss automation from Product Pitches to Brand to Deal
processes.trigger: Operator needs to test the cascading loss workflow that moves Brand and Deal to Lost when all Product Pitches are declined
processes.prompt:
Create a new company called '[TEST] Ferndale Food Hall'. They are a food hall based in Cardiff, United Kingdom. Their domain is testferndalefoodhall.com.

Then create a buyer deal called '[TEST] Ferndale Food Hall - Loss Cascade Test' in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at the Discovery stage. Associate the deal to the company. Associate the deal with the GLUG! Client Service (ID 1096053334264) so that a Brand record is auto-created.

Wait a few seconds for the Brand to be auto-created by the workflow, then find the Brand record that was created for this deal.

Now move the deal through the pipeline stages in order: Follow Up, then Feedback Pending, then Feedback Received. This triggers Product Pitch creation. Wait about 15 seconds for the Product Pitches to be automatically created.

Then move ALL Product Pitches to the Declined stage. Wait about 10 seconds for the cascading loss workflow to run.

Report what happened to the Brand stage and Deal stage after all pitches were declined.

actions.description: Create the buyer company
Create a buyer deal in the Buyer Deal Pipeline at Discovery and associate to the company with GLUG! client service
Verify a Brand record was auto-created with correct client_name_sync
Verify all Product Pitches were moved to Declined stage
Verify the Brand cascaded to Lost stage after all pitches declined
Verify the Deal cascaded to Lost stage after Brand went to Lost

teardown.description: Remove test product pitches, brand, deal, and company (pitches first via fallback, then brand, then parent records)
